claim_family: Google had direct historical links to the Transformer, PaLM, Gemini, and custom AI infrastructure before and during the public ChatGPT shock.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not claim Google invented all modern LLM progress or that technical depth guaranteed product success.

claim_family: PaLM was a 540B dense Transformer language model trained with Pathways.
allowed_in_chapter: Use the 540B and Pathways facts in prose.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not chart PaLM benchmark values or training details without separate extraction.

claim_family: Bard was publicly framed by Google as an early experiment and as complementary to Search.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for product-conversion and incumbent-search tension.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not infer adoption, user satisfaction, or market share.

claim_family: Gemini was introduced as a multimodal model family with Ultra, Pro, and Nano and as part of the Google DeepMind era.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for model-family and product-banner framing.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not use vendor benchmark claims as neutral superiority claims.

claim_family: Bard was rebranded as Gemini and paired with Gemini Advanced/mobile app experiences.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for consolidation of consumer assistant brand.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not use for revenue, subscriber growth, or broad user adoption.

claim_family: Gemini 1.5 made long context a major technical and product theme.
allowed_in_chapter: Use one-million-token consumer and two-million-token developer/cloud access-path framing with source attribution.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not claim long context solved retrieval, memory, enterprise knowledge, legal review, or codebase understanding.

claim_family: Google publicly tied Gemini training and deployment to its AI-optimized infrastructure and TPUs.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not make detailed TPU-vs-GPU performance or cost claims without hardware rows.

claim_family: Gemini 2.5 product language introduced thinking/reasoning framing before the cutoff.
allowed_in_chapter: Use as a bridge to the reasoning/test-time-compute chapter.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Exact benchmark numbers, best-model language, and cost-performance claims remain blocked.

claim_family: Google DeepMind model-card pages are useful source-discovery handles for Gemini-family documentation.
allowed_in_chapter: Use as evidence of a model-card documentation surface.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Because mutable pages can include post-cutoff updates, do not use as exact cutoff-day model list without extraction.

claim_family: AI Overviews/Search generative experience changed click behavior, publisher economics, or ad economics by specific amounts.
allowed_in_chapter: Only discuss as conceptual search-gravity tension.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Needs primary Google filings, product docs, publisher data, or reporting with date/metric normalization.

claim_family: Gemini adoption, Gemini Advanced subscriber totals, enterprise productivity impact, or cloud revenue impact.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Needs customer-side evidence, filings, or normalized third-party data.

claim_family: Gemini benchmark superiority over OpenAI/Anthropic/Meta/Chinese models.
allowed_in_chapter: Only mention that Google made model-update and thinking claims.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Requires row-level benchmark extraction and comparison methodology.
